description: Mass or Volume Air Flow Circuit Malfunction
fix_summary: Clean MAF sensor, check for intake leaks, replace sensor if readings out of spec.

description: Manifold Absolute Pressure/Barometric Pressure Circuit Malfunction
fix_summary: Check vacuum lines, test MAP sensor voltage, replace if faulty.
Check IAT sensor resistance at known temps, inspect connector, replace sensor.

common_causes: Wiring issue in coolant temp circuit
fix_summary: Check sensor connector, test sensor resistance vs temp chart, replace if out of spec.
Check TPS voltage sweep (0.5V-4.5V), verify smooth transition, replace TPS if erratic.
Check O2 sensor voltage swing (0.1V-0.9V), inspect wiring, replace sensor if lazy or stuck.
Same procedure as P0130 but for bank 2.
Check long-term fuel trim (should be ±10%), inspect for vacuum leaks, clean injectors.

common_causes: Vacuum leak (intake manifold gasket / vacuum hose)
fix_summary: Inspect intake for vacuum leaks (smoke test if available). Test MAF sensor with scan tool while cleaning if dirty. Check fuel pressure under load — should hold near 43-58 PSI on most fuel-injected bikes. Inspect EVAP purge valve.
Check fuel pressure for over-spec readings. Pull plugs and inspect for fuel-fouling. Test O2 sensor response. Inspect charcoal canister for fuel saturation.

common_causes: Faulty MAF or bank-2 O2 sensor
fix_summary: Same procedure as P0171 but focus on bank 2 intake / injectors / O2.
Same procedure as P0172 but focus on bank 2.
Check injector resistance (12-16 ohms typical), test wiring continuity, swap injectors to isolate.
Check fuel pump fuse and relay first, listen for pump prime on key-on, test voltage at pump connector.
Pressure-test charge pipe for leaks, verify wastegate actuator stroke, scope MAP/boost sensor signal during load.
Check spark plugs and coils, inspect for vacuum leaks, verify fuel pressure, compression test if needed.
Swap coil/plug with another cylinder to isolate. If misfire follows, replace that part.
Same procedure as P0301 but for cylinder 2. Swap-to-isolate is the fastest path on a 2-up V-twin.
Same swap-to-isolate procedure as P0301.
Same swap-to-isolate procedure as P0301.

description: Crankshaft Position Sensor A Circuit Malfunction
fix_summary: Check sensor gap, inspect reluctor ring for damage, test sensor resistance and AC voltage output while cranking.
Check sensor output, inspect timing chain for excessive slack, verify wiring continuity.
Clean EGR passages and valve, test valve operation with vacuum pump.
Verify O2 sensor operation first, check for exhaust leaks, replace cat if confirmed inefficient.
Same procedure as P0420 but for bank 2 cat + downstream O2.
Verify fuel cap is sealed and present. Smoke-test the EVAP system. Test purge and vent solenoid operation.

description: Evaporative Emission Control System Leak Detected (Small)
common_causes: Tiny EVAP hose crack or pinhole
fix_summary: Tighten fuel cap and clear; if returns, smoke-test EVAP. Small leaks are notoriously hard to find without smoke.

description: Evaporative Emission Control System Leak Detected (Gross)
fix_summary: Inspect fuel cap presence and seal; verify EVAP hoses are connected. Gross leaks usually find easily.
Check VSS output with scope or multimeter while spinning wheel, inspect wiring.
Clean IAC valve and throttle body, check for vacuum leaks, reset idle after cleaning.

description: Idle Control System RPM Lower Than Expected
fix_summary: Clean throttle body and IAC, inspect for vacuum leaks, check for binding throttle cable.

description: Idle Control System RPM Higher Than Expected
fix_summary: Smoke-test for vacuum leaks first (most common cause). Inspect IAC and throttle body for sticking. Verify TPS at idle position.

description: Engine Oil Pressure Sensor / Switch Range/Performance
common_causes: Actually-low oil pressure (worn pump or bearings)
fix_summary: VERIFY ACTUAL OIL PRESSURE WITH MECHANICAL GAUGE before assuming sensor failure — low real pressure will destroy a motor. Then test sensor signal vs gauge reading.
Test battery voltage (12.6V resting, 13.5-14.5V running), check stator output, inspect ground connections.
Check ECU connectors, inspect CAN bus wiring, clear code and retest — may need ECU replacement.
